title: Tier 2 — 4ème (Collège)
prompt:
You are taking a TIER 2 technical evaluation for STANDARD models. Solve 5 JavaScript exercises inspired by a middle school curriculum (4ème). Return your answers in Markdown using headers for the exercise IDs and code blocks for the JavaScript functions. [EXERCISE Math] Write a JavaScript function "pythagore(coteA, coteB)" that computes the hypotenuse using the Pythagorean theorem (sqrt(A^2 + B^2)). [EXERCISE Francais] Write a JavaScript function "conjuguerJe(verbe)" that takes a regular "-er" verb (like "chanter") and returns it conjugated with "je " in present tense (e.g. "je chante"). Just replace "er" with "e". [EXERCISE Chimie] Write a JavaScript function "melanger(couleur1, couleur2)" that returns "vert" if the colors are "bleu" and "jaune" (in any order), and "marron" otherwise. [EXERCISE Geo] Write a JavaScript function "compterPays(tableau)" that returns the length of the array. [EXERCISE React] Write a JavaScript function "SimpleComponent(props)" that simulates a React component by returning a string like "
{titre}
" where {titre} is replaced by props.titre. Expected Markdown format: ### tache_X ```javascript function solution() { ... } ``` [ALGORITHMIC EXERCISES — code pur, sans culture générale] Return each function using a Markdown header (### ) followed by a ```javascript code block. [EXERCISE algo_facile_1] Write a JavaScript function "estPalindrome(s)" that returns true if the string reads the same backward (case-sensitive). [EXERCISE algo_facile_2] Write a JavaScript function "pgcd(a, b)" that returns the greatest common divisor of a and b (both > 0). [EXERCISE algo_moyen_1] Write a JavaScript function "nombreMots(s)" that returns the number of words separated by whitespace. Ignore leading/trailing spaces. [EXERCISE algo_difficile_1] Write a JavaScript function "fusionTriee(a, b)" that merges two already-sorted arrays into one sorted array. [EXERCISE algo_defi] Write a JavaScript function "sontAnagrammes(a, b)" that returns true if a and b are anagrams (same letters rearranged, case-sensitive).

tasks.label: Algorithmique — Palindrome
tasks.hint: Comparez s à son inverse : s.split("").reverse().join("").

tasks.label: Algorithmique — PGCD
tasks.hint: Algorithme d'Euclide : tant que b !== 0, [a, b] = [b, a % b] ; renvoyez a.

tasks.label: Algorithmique — Fusion triée
tasks.hint: Deux pointeurs : comparez les têtes et prenez la plus petite à chaque pas.

tasks.label: Algorithmique — Anagrammes
tasks.hint: Triez les caractères des deux chaînes et comparez-les.
